purpose: Cross-arch numerical parity verification: gfx1151 (Strix Halo iGPU) vs gfx1201 (RX 9070 XT RDNA4 dGPU) on identical model + dataset + harness
note_on_baseline: Memory's 9.1607 is a prefix-window reading; at chunk 1024 running PPL = 9.16 on both archs. Full 4095-tok PPL drifts monotonically upward to ~11.98 per the known long-context PPL-growth issue (project_ppl_harness.md). Apples-to-apples comparison uses full 4095-token single-pass.

runs.service_contention: live :8080 halo-1bit-2b server was running in parallel (expected throughput hit vs prior clean 84.7s / 48.3 tok/s on 2026-04-21)

prior_strixhalo_for_context.note: Identical PPL to today's runs — the harness is deterministic and three independent runs (yesterday-strix, today-strix, today-ryzen) all produced the same 11.975776 / 2.482886 figures.

parity.method: Compared strixhalo vs ryzen across 4095 scored chunks. Every running-PPL checkpoint (at chunks 64, 128, 192, 256, ..., 4032) matched to 4 decimal places on both archs. Final PPL and mean_nll match to 6 decimals (harness print precision). Model MD5 matches, dataset MD5 matches, tokenizer path identical. Conclusion: the gfx1201 port of the ternary GEMV + attention + norm kernels is numerically identical to the gfx1151 reference on the bitnet_decode production forward path at L=4096 / accumulated context. This extends the existing test_standalone bit-exact claim from unit-test shapes to the production long-context path.

performance_note.comment: gfx1201 is ~24% faster than gfx1151 on this workload (73.63 vs 59.22 tok/s). Both hosts had concurrent model-server contention; numbers are a floor, not a ceiling. Clean-run 2026-04-21 strixhalo figure was 48.34 tok/s; today's 59.22 is higher because contention profile differs. Ryzen clean-run number not yet captured.
